text_content:
Welcome to Day 1 of AutoCAD Mastery!

Day 1: Introduction to AutoCAD

* Interface overview

* Setting up units and workspace

* Understanding coordinate systems

* Basic Drawing Tools (Line, Circle, Arc, Rectangle, Ellipse)

* Object snaps and grid settings

Objective: Today is about getting comfortable in the workspace and drawing your first precise shapes. You will learn how to set your units and navigate the X and Y coordinate system. By the end of this lesson, you will be able to lock onto specific points using Object Snaps and draw exact lines, circles, and rectangles.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: Precision is the absolute foundation of engineering and design. If your units are wrong, a 10-millimeter bolt hole might be drawn as 10 inches, ruining a fabrication run. You will use these skills every time you start a new file—whether you are setting up a localized millimeter workspace to design a mechanical flange, or configuring meters to map out a large-scale project site.

L - Line

C - Circle

REC - Rectangle

UN - Units

F3 - Toggle Object Snaps (OSNAP)

F7 - Toggle Grid

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Also watch 
Day 1 recap video
https://stream.mux.com/yIT92k004rxH8kC1zGE9wSHhF9R5402018qqXIR2d11B5M.m3u8

Self Introduction: https://stream.mux.com/dayRD1TRfesax0002D02UOkdEi92irl66zjqgAvLriMc9o.m3u8

Welcome to Day 2 of AutoCAD Mastery!

✏️ Day 2: Modification Commands

Move, Copy, Rotate, Mirror, isolate

Trim, Extend, Offset, Fillet, Chamfer

Objective: Drawing shapes is only half the battle; modifying them is where the real drafting happens. Today focuses on manipulating your base geometry. You will learn how to duplicate objects, round off sharp corners, mirror symmetrical parts, and cleanly trim overlapping lines to create complex profiles quickly.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: No draftsman draws a complex part perfectly on the first click. Modification commands are how you sculpt raw lines into finished products. You will use Offset to instantly create parallel walls for a floor plan, Trim to cut away intersecting piping lines in an HVAC layout, and Mirror to halve your drawing time when designing symmetrical mechanical assemblies.

M - Move

CO - Copy

RO - Rotate

MI - Mirror

TR - Trim

EX - Extend

O - Offset

F - Fillet

CHA - Chamfer

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Day 2. Core concepts:
https://stream.mux.com/n18ayTYGX8FvJEmQ7EH0002ItCB01C01gsr00fjRQyFUVE5I.m3u8

Welcome to Day 3 of AutoCAD Mastery!

🛠️ Day 3: Object Organization

Layers and layer properties

Grouping and locking objects

Objective: Professional drawings require strict organization. Today, you will master Layers. You will learn how to separate your centerlines, hidden lines, and main physical contours into easily manageable, color-coded layers.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: Without layers, a complex drawing is unreadable. When working on a multidisciplinary project, you need to be able to turn off the electrical and HVAC layers just to see the structural beams clearly. You will apply this daily to control line weights, isolate specific systems for review, and ensure your final printouts are visually organized.

LA - Layer Properties Manager

LAYISO - Isolate Layer

G - Group

UNGROUP - Ungroup objects

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 4 of AutoCAD Mastery!

🧱 Day 4: Precision & Measurement

Dimensioning tools

Using dimension styles

Measuring distances and angles

Objective: A drawing is useless without accurate measurements. Today is dedicated to communicating scale. You will apply linear and angular dimensions, measure the exact distance between points to verify your geometry, and set up a Dimension Style so your text and arrows look professional.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: The shop floor or construction team relies entirely on your numbers. A missing or unclear dimension halts production. You will use this knowledge to dimension bolt hole circles for machinists, specify the exact angle of a pipe routing, and verify clearances before equipment is ordered.

D - Dimension Style Manager

DLI - Linear Dimension

DAL - Aligned Dimension

DAN - Angular Dimension

DI - Measure Distance 

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 5 of AutoCAD Mastery!

📐 Day 5: Blocks and Reusable Content

Creating and inserting blocks

Dynamic blocks and attributes

Objective: Efficiency in drafting comes from never drawing the same thing twice. Today, you will create custom Blocks—reusable assets like mechanical fasteners, valves, or architectural doors. You will also explore Dynamic Blocks and Attributes.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: Blocks are the secret to drafting speed. Instead of redrawing a specific gate valve every time it appears in a P&ID (Piping and Instrumentation Diagram), you will create it once as a Block and drop it in instantly. Attributes allow you to embed part numbers and supplier data directly into that block, which can later be extracted to automate your material purchasing lists.

B - Create Block

I - Insert Block

BEDIT - Block Editor

ATTDEF - Define Attribute 

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 6 of AutoCAD Mastery!
🧩 Day 6: Practice Project 1

Simple floor plan or mechanical part

Apply all tools learned so far

Objective: It is time to put the theory into practice. Today is a pure execution day. You will follow along to build a complete 2D floor plan or a detailed mechanical component from scratch, utilizing every drafting, modification, and organization tool covered from Day 1 to Day 5.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: Software proficiency is built through muscle memory. By combining all the disparate tools into a single workflow, you transition from knowing what a button does to knowing when to use it to solve a real drafting problem efficiently.

Ctrl + S - Save your progress frequently

Z then E - Zoom Extents

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 7 of AutoCAD Mastery!

🖼️ Day 7: Hatching & Gradients

Hatch patterns and boundary detection

Gradient fills and customization

Objective: Today focuses on material representation. You will use Hatching to indicate cutaway sections, distinct materials, or specific aesthetic patterns. You will learn how to manage hatch boundaries so your patterns fill perfectly without spilling over.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: In mechanical engineering, a cross-section drawing requires hatching to show which parts are solid metal and which are hollow cavities. In civil or architectural work, you need different hatches to distinguish between concrete, earth, and steel. This knowledge ensures your 2D drawings visually communicate the physical materials of the final product.

H - Hatch

HE - Hatch Edit

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 8 of AutoCAD Mastery!

🗓️ Week 2: Intermediate Skills & Output
🎨 Day 8: Annotation & Text

Multiline text and single-line text

Leaders and tables

Objective: Clear communication is just as critical as precise geometry. Today, you will add shop floor notes, titles, and specifications using Multiline text. You will also learn how to create Leaders to point out specific details and format Tables.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: Drawings are legal documents. The geometry shows the shape, but the text tells the contractor how to build it. You will use these tools to add critical welding specifications, material grades, and generate clean Bills of Materials (BOM) tables so the procurement team knows exactly what to buy.

MT or T - Multiline Text

LE - Quick Leader

TB - Table

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 9 of AutoCAD Mastery!

🗂️ Day 9: 3D Modeling in AutoCAD

Understanding 3D workspace and switching environments

World Coordinate System (WCS) vs User Coordinate System (UCS)

Creating basic 3D objects

Orbit, pan, zoom, and creating custom views

Objective: We are breaking out of the 2D plane. Today, you will manipulate the User Coordinate System (UCS) so you can draw on any face of a 3D object. You will then extrude 2D profiles into solid 3D models and navigate around them fluidly.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: 2D drawings can hide spatial conflicts (clashes). By modeling a mechanical assembly or a pump room in 3D, you can visually verify that pipes don't intersect with structural columns before a single piece of steel is cut. Mastering the UCS allows you to model complex geometries on any angled plane.

EXT - Extrude

UCS - Manage User Coordinate System

3DO - 3D Orbit

VPORTS - Viewports configuration

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 10 of AutoCAD Mastery!

🧭 Day 10: Reading Existing Drawings

Open and review the drawing layout and layers

Check object properties for accuracy

Edit geometry with basic modification commands

Objective: In a real-world environment, you rarely start with a blank screen. Today, you will learn how to open a massive client drawing, audit its layer structure, navigate complex layouts without breaking them, and confidently make modifications to an existing engineering file.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: When you join an ongoing project at an engineering firm, you will inherit drawings from other designers or contractors. You need to know how to safely navigate a heavy, complex master site plan, locate the specific mechanical zone you are responsible for, and extract the data you need without accidentally deleting the architect's gridlines.

PR or CH - Open Properties Palette

LAYWALK - Walk through layers to inspect them

ID - Find coordinate point

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 11 of AutoCAD Mastery!

🧮 Day 11: Layouts & Viewports

Model space vs paper space

Creating and scaling viewports

Applying materials, lighting, and rendering 3D views

Objective: Today bridges the gap between the model and the final presentation. You will switch to Paper Space to set up your title blocks and create scaled Viewports. For the 3D side, you will apply basic materials and configure lighting for a realistic render.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: The Model Space is for you to design; the Paper Space is for the client to view. You will use Viewports to take a single 3D model and create multiple scaled 2D views on a single sheet (e.g., an isometric view next to a top-down plan). This is the exact workflow used to generate professional, multi-page construction drawing sets.

MS - Switch to Model Space

PS - Switch to Paper Space

MV - Make Viewport

RR - Render

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 12 of AutoCAD Mastery!

🖨️ Day 12: Plotting & Printing

Page setup and plot styles

Exporting to PDF and print settings

Objective: Your design is finished; now it needs to be issued. Today covers Page Setups and Plot Styles (CTB files) to ensure your line weights print correctly. You will export your layouts to perfectly scaled PDFs ready for fabrication.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: If a drawing prints with all lines at the exact same thickness, it is impossible to read. Plot styles ensure that main object lines print bold and dark, while dimension lines and hatching print thin and light. This guarantees your digital work translates into a highly professional, readable physical blueprint.

PLOT or Ctrl+P - Open Plot Dialog

PAGESETUP - Page Setup Manager

PREVIEW - Plot Preview

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 13 of AutoCAD Mastery!

🧾 Day 13: Introduction to AI as your Competent Assistant

Proper Prompting using AI

Objective: Drafting software is complex, but you don't have to memorize everything. Today, you will learn how to effectively prompt an AI to troubleshoot stubborn AutoCAD errors or look up industry-standard dimensions.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: Modern engineers leverage AI to solve bottlenecks instantly. Instead of spending two hours on a forum trying to figure out why a hatch won't close, or manually counting 200 light fixtures, you can prompt an AI to explain the error or write a custom AutoLISP script to automate the counting for you, saving massive amounts of billable time.

(Focus is on browser navigation and effective text prompting techniques)

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!

Welcome to Day 14 of AutoCAD Mastery!

🏁 Day 14: Final Project & Review

Capstone project (e.g., house plan or machine part)

Review key concepts and Q&A (utilize AI support)

Objective: This is your capstone. You will take a project from a blank screen to a fully dimensioned, layered, and plotted PDF. We will review the core commands, and you will use your new AI prompting skills to troubleshoot any roadblocks.

Why It Matters & Real-World Application: Completing this capstone gives you a tangible asset. You will walk away with a professional-grade drawing package that you can immediately include in your portfolio to prove your AutoCAD competency to prospective clients or employers.

(Use all previous shortcuts to complete the final deliverable)

At the end of the lesson, there is a 5-question quiz to test your knowledge. You must pass with 80% (4/5 correct) to unlock the next day!
